response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings strong polyglot engineering breadth and over 15 years of hands-on experience, including genuinely high-scale systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic processing millions of HTTP requests per second. However, the role's core requirements — experimentation platform ownership, feature flagging, telemetry infrastructure, A/B testing frameworks, statistical bias detection, and rollout-risk management — are entirely absent from the resume. There is no evidence of growth engineering work, experiment assignment systems, metrics pipeline design, or cross-team experimentation tooling, which are the foundational competencies this Principal-level role demands. The candidate's current trajectory (AI agent tooling, full-stack product work at a small consultancy) does not align with the enterprise-scale experimentation systems leadership this position requires.
strengths: Genuine high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of req/s, async I/O, low-latency micro-services) maps to the reliability and scale expectations of the role
Polyglot proficiency across JavaScript/TypeScript, C++, C#, Java, and DLang satisfies the language breadth requirements, and 15+ years of tenure clearly exceeds the 8-year threshold

Demonstrated engineering craftsmanship culture (TDD advocacy, open-source test tooling, teaching and mentoring through workshops) aligns with the hands-on technical leadership and mentoring expectations
concerns: Zero evidence of experimentation platform work: no A/B testing systems, feature flagging, experiment assignment/targeting, rollout automation, or bias detection — the preferred qualification asks for 4+ years of this, and the resume shows none

No telemetry or metrics infrastructure experience: the role requires building telemetry foundations and analysis workflows, but the resume contains no instrumentation, observability pipeline, or growth measurement work

Current and recent roles (re:cinq staff engineer, Optilyz pricing team) are generalist or domain-specific rather than platform-oriented, with no evidence of cross-team technical influence or ownership of systems used by multiple teams at scale

key_factors.factor: No telemetry or growth metrics work
